title: Metastelma huberi
intro: Metastelma huberi, also known as Huber's metaphyl, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. Traditionally, Metastelma huberi has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with its leaves and stems said to possess health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and combating infections. The bioactive constituents of Metastelma huberi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Metastelma huberi is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the latter being a concentrated liquid extract often used in pharmaceutical applications.
taxonomy: Metastelma huberi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is part of the genus Metastelma, which consists of various species of twining plants native to tropical regions. Metastelma huberi specifically is native to Central and South America. The classification of Metastelma huberi places it within the order Gentianales. The plant's classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Apocynaceae family.
